assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md, the Verification Summary table shows: '| Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality WARN: 91% pass rate, 2 regression failures in eval-3. No test files in diff (Repetitive Test Detection: N/A, Test Documentation: N/A) |'. The Test Quality verdict is WARN, and the details explicitly state the cause is 'Eval Quality WARN' due to eval-3's 2 regression failures.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: The report.md contains an 'Eval Results' section with a table showing 5 evals with pass/fail counts and pass rates (eval-1 through eval-5). The Test Quality row states 'Eval Quality WARN: 91% pass rate, 2 regression failures in eval-3'. The eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertion counts) are extracted and presented, confirming the eval review was detected and processed. Note: the review-40002.md file shows reviewer-b (not github-actions[bot]) as the author, but the eval data is clearly processed in the report with detailed per-eval breakdowns and the eval-3 regression analysis.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md classifies comment 50001 from reviewer-b as a 'code change request' based on the reviewer's language and CHANGES_REQUESTED state. The classification reasoning discusses the reviewer's intent, the gap in Markdown exclusion, and the specific code modification requested. There is zero mention of eval detection, eval metrics, pass rates, or assertions. It is processed purely as a normal review comment with standard classification logic (code change request vs. suggestion analysis).

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: The Test Quality row in report.md's Verification Summary states: '| Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality WARN: 91% pass rate, 2 regression failures in eval-3. No test files in diff (Repetitive Test Detection: N/A, Test Documentation: N/A) |'. While it mentions the pass rate (91%) and the count of regression failures (2 in eval-3), it does NOT include the specific failing assertion details about 'convention upgrade eligibility' and 'sub-task creation' in the Test Quality row itself. Those details appear in the 'Eval-3 regressions' note below the Eval Results table ('Convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation for suggestion-classified review comments'), but not in the Test Quality row as asserted.

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-2.md is titled 'Fix eval-3 assertion regressions: convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation'. Its Description states: 'Fix two eval-3 assertion regressions introduced by the Documentation Coverage changes.' The Implementation Notes section lists both failing assertions with their full text and evidence. The Sub-Tasks Created table in report.md confirms: '| 2 | Fix eval-3 assertion regressions: convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation | Eval failure | Eval-3 regression (2 assertions) |'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-2.md contains a '## Review Context' section with 'Eval result review excerpt (from CI eval run):' followed by both failing assertions quoted in full. Assertion 1: 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion)...' with Evidence: 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility -- no CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis is documented in the classification reasoning'. Assertion 2: 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path...' with Evidence: 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002 -- it was classified as suggestion and no convention upgrade was attempted, so the suggestion was not elevated to a code change request'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: Both subtask-1.md and subtask-2.md contain a '## Target PR' section with the URL 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. subtask-1.md line 41: '## Target PR\nhttps://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. subtask-2.md line 42: '## Target PR\nhttps://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: The report.md Verification Summary table does not contain a 'Root-Cause Investigation' row or verdict. The table rows are: Review Feedback, Scope Containment, Diff Size, Commit Traceability, Sensitive Patterns, CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, Test Quality, Test Change Classification, Verification Commands. There is no Root-Cause Investigation entry anywhere in the report.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The output directory contains only: criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md (acceptance criteria evaluations), report.md (verification report), review-40002.md and review-50001.md (review classifications), and subtask-1.md and subtask-2.md (sub-task descriptions). All outputs are verification artifacts (reports, classifications, sub-task descriptions). No code files (.ts, .js, .py, .rs, .go, .md skill files, etc.) were modified. The report itself notes the PR modifies 'style-conventions.md' and 'SKILL.md' but the skill's outputs are separate description/report files, not modifications to those code files.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md: Scope Containment is PASS with details 'PR modifies exactly the 2 files specified in the task (style-conventions.md, SKILL.md)' -- this compares diff files against task's Files to Modify with no reference to eval results. Sensitive Patterns is PASS with details 'No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns in the diff' -- this scans the diff for secrets independently. Acceptance Criteria is PASS with '7/7 acceptance criteria satisfied' -- each criterion (criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md) evaluates the PR diff content against specific acceptance criteria about Check 6 behavior, with no references to eval results in any criterion file. All three checks operate on their own inputs (diff files, task spec, criterion definitions) without any cross-contamination from eval processing.
